test_cases.description: packageMessage produces a padded Uint8Array for a public message with correct structure
test_cases.input_description: A non-empty content string with type='public', no channelId or recipientPubKey
test_cases.expected_output_description: A Uint8Array whose length is a power of 2 and <= 65536, containing a JSON message with ttl, from.pubKey, and a valid signature
assertions: Result is a Uint8Array
Result length is a power of 2
Result length <= 65536
Deserialized message has ttl field
Deserialized message has from.pubKey
Deserialized message has a non-empty signature

test_cases.description: packageMessage for type='private' omits from.pubKey and sets a 16-char pseudonym
test_cases.input_description: Content string with type='private' and a valid channelId
test_cases.expected_output_description: Padded Uint8Array containing JSON where from.pubKey is absent and from.name is 16 characters
assertions: Result is a Uint8Array
Deserialized message does not have from.pubKey
Deserialized message from.name is exactly 16 characters

test_cases.description: packageMessage for type='direct' includes from.pubKey in the serialized message
test_cases.input_description: Content string with type='direct' and a recipientPubKey
test_cases.expected_output_description: Padded Uint8Array containing JSON with from.pubKey present
assertions: Result is a Uint8Array
Deserialized message has from.pubKey present
Deserialized message has a non-empty signature

test_cases.description: packageMessage throws missing_channel_id when type='private' but channelId is not provided
test_cases.input_description: Content string with type='private', channelId=undefined
assertions: Function throws an error when channelId is missing for private type

test_cases.description: packageMessage throws message_too_long when content exceeds max padded frame size
test_cases.input_description: A very large content string (>64KB after serialization)
assertions: Function throws a RangeError for oversized messages

test_cases.description: handleMessageSubmit calls preventDefault synchronously, broadcasts the padded frame, and clears the input
test_cases.input_description: A mocked DOM event with preventDefault method and a UI input field with content
test_cases.expected_output_description: preventDefault called, broadcastMessage called with Uint8Array, input field cleared
assertions: event.preventDefault() was called
broadcastMessage was called with a Uint8Array argument
UI input field value is empty after submission

test_cases.description: handleMessageSubmit displays UI warning when packageMessage throws RangeError (message too long)
test_cases.input_description: A mocked DOM event with a very large message in the input field
assertions: event.preventDefault() was called synchronously
appendMessage was called with a warning containing 'Message too long'

test_cases.description: handleMessageSubmit logs unexpected errors via console.error
test_cases.input_description: A mocked DOM event where packageMessage throws a non-RangeError
assertions: event.preventDefault() was called
console.error was called with the error

test_cases.description: handleMessageSubmit calls event.preventDefault() BEFORE any async operation
test_cases.input_description: A mocked DOM event that tracks call order
test_cases.expected_output_description: preventDefault is the first call made before any await
assertions: preventDefault was called synchronously before any async work

test_cases.description: broadcastMessage sends a Uint8Array as binary to all connected peers
test_cases.input_description: A Uint8Array frame and multiple mock WebSocket peers
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each peer's ws.send() receives the exact same Uint8Array
assertions: ws.send() was called on each connected peer
The argument to send() is the original Uint8Array (no transformation)

test_cases.description: broadcastMessage sends a string frame as text to all connected peers (backward compatibility)
test_cases.input_description: A string frame and multiple mock WebSocket peers
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each peer's ws.send() receives the exact string
assertions: ws.send() was called on each connected peer with the string

test_cases.description: broadcastMessage handles send_failure gracefully when one peer's WebSocket throws
test_cases.input_description: A frame and multiple peers where one peer's ws.send() throws
assertions: Other peers still receive the message
Error is handled without crashing

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame processes a valid binary frame and delivers it to the message handler callback
test_cases.input_description: A valid padded binary frame containing a properly signed, non-expired, non-duplicate message
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns 'delivered' and message handler callback is called
assertions: Return value is 'delivered'
Message handler callback was called with the deserialized message

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame routes text frames matching isProtocolMessage to the discovery handler
test_cases.input_description: A text (string) WebSocket frame that isProtocolMessage returns true for
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns 'protocol_routed' and discovery handler is invoked
assertions: Return value is 'protocol_routed'
Discovery handler was called
Message handler callback was NOT called

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame drops malformed binary frames and logs via console.error
test_cases.input_description: An ArrayBuffer containing random non-JSON bytes
assertions: Return value is 'malformed_dropped'
console.error was called

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame drops expired messages with console.warn
test_cases.input_description: A valid padded frame where the TTL timestamp indicates expiry
assertions: Return value is 'expired_dropped'
console.warn was called
Message handler callback was NOT called

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame silently drops duplicate messages
test_cases.input_description: A valid padded frame with a message ID that already exists in the dedup set
assertions: Return value is 'duplicate_dropped'
Message handler callback was NOT called

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame drops messages with from.pubKey but invalid signature, logs console.warn
test_cases.input_description: A padded frame with a valid structure but tampered signature bytes
assertions: Return value is 'signature_failed_dropped'
console.warn was called
Message handler callback was NOT called

test_cases.description: handleIncomingFrame drops messages that have from.pubKey but missing/empty signature
test_cases.input_description: A padded frame with from.pubKey present but signature field empty
assertions: Return value is 'pubkey_without_signature_dropped'
console.warn was called
Message handler callback was NOT called

test_cases.description: Channel messages (no from.pubKey) skip signature verification entirely
test_cases.input_description: A padded frame for a channel message (type='private') with no from.pubKey
test_cases.expected_output_description: Message is delivered without calling verifySignature
assertions: Return value is 'delivered'
verifySignature was never called
Message handler callback was called

test_cases.description: Valid messages are rebroadcast using the ORIGINAL raw frame bytes, not re-encoded
test_cases.input_description: A valid padded binary frame
test_cases.expected_output_description: broadcastMessage is called with the exact same ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array that was received
assertions: broadcastMessage was called
The argument to broadcastMessage is reference-equal or byte-equal to the original rawFrame

test_cases.description: A public message packaged by sender can be received and delivered by receiver with content integrity
test_cases.input_description: packageMessage output for a public message fed into handleIncomingFrame on a different peer
test_cases.expected_output_description: Receiver delivers the message with the same content string
assertions: handleIncomingFrame returns 'delivered'
Delivered message content matches the original content string

test_cases.description: A channel (private) message can be packaged and received successfully, skipping signature verification
test_cases.input_description: packageMessage output for type='private' with channelId, fed into handleIncomingFrame
test_cases.expected_output_description: Receiver delivers the message; from.pubKey is absent
assertions: handleIncomingFrame returns 'delivered'
Delivered message does not have from.pubKey

test_cases.description: Multiple calls to packageMessage produce unique message IDs
test_cases.input_description: Call packageMessage multiple times with identical content
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each resulting message has a distinct id field
assertions: All message IDs are unique across calls

test_cases.description: getConnectedPeerCount returns the number of connected peers and is >= 0
test_cases.input_description: Network module with some peers connected
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns an integer equal to the number of connected peers
assertions: Return value >= 0
Return value matches the actual number of connected peers

test_cases.description: getConnectedPeerUrls returns a new array (not internal state reference) with correct URLs
test_cases.input_description: Network module with known peer URLs
test_cases.expected_output_description: A fresh array containing all peer URLs; mutating it does not affect internal state
assertions: Return value is an array
Length matches getConnectedPeerCount()
Mutating the returned array does not change a subsequent call result

test_cases.description: getConnectedPeerCount returns 0 when no peers are connected
test_cases.input_description: Network module with an empty peers Map
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returns 0
assertions: Return value is 0

test_cases.description: initializeWebSocket sets binaryType to 'arraybuffer' on the created WebSocket
test_cases.input_description: A valid ws:// URL
test_cases.expected_output_description: Returned WebSocket has binaryType === 'arraybuffer'
assertions: ws.binaryType is 'arraybuffer'

test_cases.description: initializeWebSocket handles connection_failed when WebSocket constructor throws
test_cases.input_description: An invalid URL that causes WebSocket constructor to throw
assertions: Function throws or rejects when WebSocket creation fails

test_cases.description: initializeDiscoveryBootstrap constructs NetworkAPI and passes it to initializeDiscovery after network init
test_cases.input_description: Network module already initialized with exports available
test_cases.expected_output_description: initializeDiscovery is called with an object containing getConnectedPeerCount, getConnectedPeerUrls, broadcastMessage, connectToPeer
assertions: initializeDiscovery was called once
The argument has all four NetworkAPI methods

test_cases.description: initializeDiscoveryBootstrap fails if called before initializeNetwork
test_cases.input_description: Network module not yet initialized
assertions: Function throws or returns an error indicating network is not initialized

test_cases.description: Channel messages (type='private') never contain from.pubKey in the serialized package
test_cases.input_description: packageMessage with type='private' and valid channelId
test_cases.expected_output_description: The serialized JSON inside the padded frame does not contain from.pubKey
assertions: from.pubKey is undefined/absent in the deserialized message

test_cases.description: Padded frames always have a length that is a power of 2
test_cases.input_description: Various message content sizes
test_cases.expected_output_description: Each padded frame length is a power of 2
assertions: Frame length is a power of 2 for all tested sizes

test_cases.description: Security-relevant drops (expired, bad signature) use console.warn, not console.error
test_cases.input_description: Frames that trigger expired and signature_failed drops
test_cases.expected_output_description: console.warn is called, console.error is not called for these cases
assertions: console.warn was called for expired messages
console.warn was called for failed signature messages
console.error was NOT called for these security drops

test_cases.description: Text frames route to discovery; binary frames route to user message pipeline — discrimination happens first
test_cases.input_description: One text frame and one binary frame
test_cases.expected_output_description: Text frame goes to protocol handler; binary frame goes to message pipeline
assertions: Text frame triggers protocol/discovery handler
Binary frame triggers message pipeline processing
